title: Forgeframe Studios Launches New Cinematic Reel for 2025
content:
We're excited to share our latest studio reel, showcasing a year of commercial, corporate, and documentary work. The reel highlights our range—from high-energy product spots to intimate documentary storytelling—and underscores our commitment to craft and collaboration.

Every project in the reel was produced in-house, from concept through color and sound. We're grateful to the brands and creators who trusted us with their stories and look forward to what's next.
excerpt: Our 2025 studio reel is live, featuring commercial, corporate, and documentary work from the past year.

title: Why Color Grading Matters for Brand Consistency
content:
Color grading isn't just about making footage "look nice"—it's a powerful tool for brand consistency and emotional tone. When your videos share a cohesive look, audiences subconsciously associate that look with your brand. In this post, we break down how we approach color for commercial and corporate projects: from matching shots across a campaign to creating custom LUTs that can be reused across future work.

We'll also touch on common pitfalls, like over-grading or ignoring delivery specs, and how to avoid them. Whether you're planning a single spot or a full campaign, investing in thoughtful color pays off.
excerpt: How color grading supports brand consistency and emotional tone—and what to avoid.

title: Behind the Scenes: Event Videography at Scale
content:
Last quarter we covered a multi-day conference with three camera teams, live switching, and same-day highlight edits. In this behind-the-scenes look, we share how we plan large-scale event videography: shot lists, communication protocols, backup gear, and post workflows that let us turn around a 3-minute highlight within 24 hours.

If you're considering event coverage for a conference, product launch, or gala, this post offers practical tips on scope, crew, and deliverables so you can brief your team—or your vendor—with confidence.
excerpt: How we planned and executed multi-day conference coverage with three teams and same-day highlights.
